name: Eddie Safranski
abstract: Eddie Safranski (December 25, 1918 – January 10, 1974) was an American jazz double bassist, composer and arranger who worked with Stan Kenton. He also worked with Tony Bennett, Charlie Barnet, Benny Goodman and Bobby Darin.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic

works.t: The New Moon: Lover, Come Back to Me

concerts.label: Anahid Ajemian, Violin, and Maro Ajemian, Piano

works.t: Suite for Violin, Piano, and Small Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Lesberg
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Safranski and I were the two hot guys, and they would add us to the other six who were playing with the NBC Orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Lesberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I had Eddie Safranski and Don Lamond... The first time I had Eddie Safranski... So they took this gig with me
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: And Eddie Safranski was playing bass... And we had... Claude Williamson was the piano player.

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was known at that time as the NBC rhythm section because they were all on staff. That was Mondello, Dick Hyman, Don Lamond and Eddie Safranski. And we just went in and played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998
